dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3839 IS: Ratepayer Affordability and Transparency in Energy Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources

form.official-title: To protect the reliability of the electric grid by preempting certain State climate mandates, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Ratepayer Affordability and Transparency in Energy Act of 2026.

The purpose of this Act is to safeguard the reliability of the electric grid by preempting State climate mandates, including renewable portfolio standards, that obstruct or distort energy infrastructure planning, inflate electricity costs, or otherwise imperil grid reliability.

paragraph.text: The term State law includes any constitution, statute, regulation, rule, ordinance, charter, order, or other authority of—

subparagraph.text: any political subdivision of a State.
the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and
any other territory or possession of the United States.

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no State, political subdivision of a State, or regulatory authority may establish, enforce, or continue in effect any requirement, standard, mandate, or program that—

paragraph.text: requires electricity generation, retail electricity sales, or electricity procurement to include a specified percentage or quantity of electricity derived from renewable, zero-emission, or carbon-free energy resources; or
conditions participation in wholesale or retail electricity markets, cost recovery, or utility regulation on compliance with a requirement described in paragraph (1).

subsection.text: Any State law or other requirement that is inconsistent with subsection (a) is hereby preempted and shall have no force or effect.

Nothing in this section prohibits a State, political subdivision of a State, or regulatory authority, as applicable, from owning or operating generation facilities that use renewable, zero-emission, or carbon-free energy resources.
